Natir is a tall servant from the coastal city of Dalilak who serves as the steward for Roshone following the death of Miliv. He is accompanied by his wife, Alaxia, and is responsible for managing the household affairs of the lighteyes Roshone.
Dalilak is a large coastal city located to the north, from which the steward Natir was brought after the death of Miliv.
Cabine is a character described as one of the ten fools who acts like a child despite being an adult.
Rillir is the son of Roshone who enters the kitchens chatting with a companion, expressing dissatisfaction with his father's provincial lifestyle and desire for hunting trips.

Rillir Roshone is the son of Roshone, a lighteyes master who views the remote location as provincial and desires to take his son on hunts. He is the antagonist Kal addresses, challenging him to a confrontation regarding his father's treatment of Kal's father.
A social caste or status within the city of Salas that denotes a low position, comparable to being demoted to the tenth dahn, and prevents an individual from inheriting through marriage.
Gashash-son-Navammis is a character whose translation is considered uncertain by the narrator, who intends to verify a quote attributed to him in the fourteenth book of Seld.

Goshel is a slave who was led in a rebellion by the protagonist, an event that resulted in the group being slaughtered. He is one of several individuals the protagonist has failed to save, including Tien, Dallet, and a nameless slave.
The Envisagers are a group whose existence is known only through stories, legends, and idle dreams, representing a subject of significant fear and rejection by Teft.
Alezarv is a character who possesses the ability to cross a distance in a heartbeat that would normally take more than four months to travel by foot.
Calinam is the author of the folktale 'Among the Darkeyed,' which is recorded on page 102 and features stories of instantaneous travel and the Oathgates.
Oathgates are a form of instantaneous travel featured in folktales recorded by Calinam.

A philosophical concept asserting that morality is distinct from human ideals and exists as a whole to be approached but never fully understood by mortals. It posits that removing evil is ultimately moral, thereby justifying the destruction of evil men.
Sur Kamar is a person who attended a feast in the narrator's father's name, an event the narrator felt compelled to attend despite the travel required.

Bluebar is the name of a brand of jam produced by Brother Kabsal, which is given to a recipient with a note suggesting that enjoying it indicates the recipient is mysterious, reserved, and thoughtful.
Ambrian is the author of the book 'The Last Desolation', as indicated by the attribution in the provided text.
Idolir is a person mentioned alongside Treff in a conversation with Kaladin regarding a crew of men who have lost track of the weeks.
Treff is a member of a crew of men who, along with Idolir, are questioned by Kaladin regarding their inability to keep track of the weeks.

Marabethia is a location situated beside the sea in the northern Selay lands, known for its inhabitants' passion for public debate at city intersections and their custom of carrying overripe fruit to counter arguments.
Brightlord Rillir is the son of Brightlord Roshone who is being treated by Lirin for severe injuries sustained from bony spears, including a crushed leg and an object protruding from his abdomen.
Alds is a townsman who was on a hunt with Milp but was left behind by Roshone while he rushed to assist the wounded lighteyes.
Milp is a townsman who was on a hunt alongside Alds but was left behind by Roshone when he departed to travel quickly and attend to Rillir.
Cormshen is a character who speaks about the consequences of darkness, stating that wherever it lurks, light is taken away and skin is burned.
Innia is a character who records children's folktales, specifically describing the Voidbringers as recurring threats that arrive like a highstorm and referring to their appearances as the Desolation.
The Order of Stonewards is a faction whose members are described as being able to transform like shadows, a nature observed in a scrap collected from Talatin, a Radiant of the Order.
New Natanan is a location referenced in records collected by hunters, explorers, and tradesmen who were searching for a river passage to reach it.

Elhokar Kholin is the king's son and heir who rules the feast in his father's absence. He is criticized by his superiors for allowing his sister to use a fabrial, a device considered trivially important compared to Soulcasters.
Vorin Roshar is a geographical region where the repercussions of stealing a specific fabrial could be felt, encompassing the kingdoms of Alethkar and Kharbranth.
Brightness Hashal is a character who serves as the new captain alongside her husband, Brightlord Matal, and acts as an experiment to utilize parshmen as bridgemen.
Brightlord Matal is the husband of Brightness Hashal and serves as the new captain of the group. He is described as a tall, spindly man with delicate hands who has not practiced with his sword much.
Matal is a tall, spindly lighteye who serves as a bridge crew leader and Hashal's husband, tasked with directing the assault and managing the crossing of the chasm.
Durk is a character who served as a mentor to Kaladin, offering him advice on gambling and superstition, and previously referred to Kaladin as a natural-born soldier.
Yulay is a location where groups of people discuss the Radiants and express a desire for their return.
Highmarshal Amaram is a renowned soldier and head of Alethi defense in the area, who appears to welcome a warlord and leads an army of six thousand men preparing for battle.
Waber is a character who is tasked by Roshone to gather the townspeople for a full town meeting and is present in the square to help erect a canopy as the rain intensifies.
Alaxia is the stern wife of Natir, a steward who accompanies him to assist Roshone in recruiting young men for Amaram's undermanned forces.
Callins is a farmer who addresses a noble as Brightlord and questions the necessity of a specific duty.
Abry is a character who speaks in a conversation, agreeing with a previous statement.
Agil is a character identified as the son of Marf, listed alongside Caull, son of Taleb, on a roster read by Alaxia.
Marf. Caull is the father of Agil, as indicated by the lineage description 'Agil, son of Marf. Caull, son of Taleb' read by Alaxia.
Taleb is the father of Caull, who is listed alongside Agil, son of Marf, on a document reviewed by Alaxia.
Habrin is the son of Arafik, listed alongside Jorna and Tien as individuals introduced by Alaxia.
Arafik is the father of Habrin, who is listed alongside other individuals such as Jorna and Tien in a group introduced by Alaxia.
Jorna is a character introduced as the son of Loats, listed alongside other individuals such as Habrin and Tien during a formal introduction.
Loats is the father of Jorna, who is listed alongside Habrin and Tien as a son of his respective father in a sequence of names.
Hearth-stone is the location where Roshone remains trapped after seeking revenge, despite the death of his son.

Yelig-nar, also known as Blightwind, is a character who possesses the ability to speak like a man but is accompanied by the wails of those he consumes. He is a destructive force responsible for slaughtering wordsmen and breaking into the chancery, contributing to the near-destruction of the world and the loss of half the members of many families.
Blightwind is the name given to Yelig-nar, a being capable of speaking like a man whose voice is frequently accompanied by the wails of those he consumes.
Traxil is the author of a quote regarding the Unmade, which is considered a primary source, though its authenticity is doubted.
Klade is a character who frequently asks about the Parshendi's music and expresses a carefree attitude toward highstorms destroying their crude crem and stone buildings.
Stormwarden Matain is a scholar who accompanied the king and dictated a record describing the first Parshendi hunting party encountered by the group.
Matain is a source of an account regarding a man's interest in the Parshendi, their language, society, and music before he could have known about their Shardblades.
The Vanrial is an order of artists who reside on the slopes of the Silent Mount in Jah Keved and are known for singing ancient songs that may have been written in the Dawnchant by the Heralds.
Tifandor is the subject of a biography referenced by Shallan, who seeks a copy of the book while searching for the author in the library.
Aharietiam is the name of the final Desolation, an event where the Voidbringers were cast back into the Tranquiline Halls, ending the Heraldic Epochs and beginning the Era of Solitude.

The ardentia is a group or organization within the society described, where membership status determines an individual's social standing and economic position, as leaving the ardentia results in severe demotion and loss of resources.
Veden City is a location that the narrator was scheduled to visit for dinner, situated along a route that includes the city of Kholinar and the fortress of Urithiru.
Tivbet is a person whom the narrator insists on visiting in Kholinar before traveling to Veden City, despite the growing unreasonableness of tariffs through Urithiru.
The Radiants are a group of individuals whose true nature has been revealed, though the text does not specify their exact identity or abilities beyond this revelation.

Terxim is the author of an autobiography from which only a single page remains after the original Palanaeum was fired. This surviving page is the only line of information currently useful to the narrator.
The Sea of Spears is a shallow inland sea located north and west of Alethkar, characterized by broken fingers of rock that jut above the water's surface.
Tanavast is a location referenced in the phrase 'Child of Tanavast,' which appears to be a title or lineage associated with the character Kaladin.

Babatharnam is a land located in the southwestern region of Roshar, known for its peculiar trees that lie down when a highstorm approaches and a population of Babath who are particular about speech and possess distinctive vein patterns beneath their skin. The region is currently experiencing significant unrest and danger, where the Monavakahs ensure their family members live the longest while others face assassination, exile, or death.
Babab is a person referenced in conversation by Moash, though their specific identity or role is not defined within the provided text.
Babath is a location where a unique system of rule exists, granting office to the elderly and designating the oldest ruler as the Most Ancient.
A title used to describe a person who visits places around the land and tells others of what they have seen, a role that Rock identifies as the correct name for a specific type of individual.
Delp is a member of Kaladin's bridge crew who was wounded in the neck by an arrow during a previous run, leaving the team with only twenty-eight bridge-capable members.
A term used by Kaladin to address one of the bridgemen, indicating that the group he is speaking to is composed of individuals identified by this specific name.
A lordling character who appears to be only a few years older than Kaladin.
Reral Makoram is a Brightlord who commands the Sixth Battalion and is contacted by a Shardbearer to inform him that Adolin Kholin cannot attend a scheduled meeting.
A military unit commanded by Brightlord Reral Makoram, to whom Kaladin is instructed to deliver a message regarding Adolin Kholin's absence from a scheduled meeting.
Adolin Kholin is a Shardbearer who sends a message to Kaladin stating that he will not attend a scheduled meeting and will arrange to reschedule it later.
Merem is a word inscribed on a banner flying before a rising sun, paired with the word khakh to represent the concepts of honor and determination.
Khakh is a soldier who serves alongside Merem under the banner of Amaram, representing the ideals of honor and determination.
Nalem is a sergeant who serves under the squadleader Gare, accompanying him alongside another sergeant named Korabet.
Korabet is one of the two sergeants who follow the squadleader Gare, alongside the other sergeant named Nalem.
Galan is a character who identifies a new recruit named Cenn to Kaladin, who has just joined the group the previous day.

The Nightwatcher is a mysterious entity that appears to toy with the enemy forces, occasionally setting them as allies against the Vedens or Reshi and then turning them against one another, while also being a possible source of visions alongside the Voidbringers.
Ven is the chief of the surgeons, a tall man with a bulbous nose and a long face who oversees the surgical pavilion and accepts bribes to prioritize certain soldiers for battlefield attention.
Larn is a character who fought alongside Kaladin and reacted with surprise when Kaladin broke formation to evade enemy attacks.
Ko-rater is a person addressed alongside Cyn by Kaladin to return with the boy, indicating they are part of the group of soldiers currently present.
Toorim is a short man who was rescued by Kaladin and serves in his squad, where he covertly searches for spheres during combat and reports on the status of fallen soldiers.
Norby is the captainlord of the group that Kaladin belongs to, whom Kaladin intends to summon to verify their kill.
Lyndel is a character who was trampled by the Shardbearer after the Shardbearer cut down Cyn, resulting in her face being smashed in.
Raksha is a member of Kaladin's squad who is killed by the Shardbearer alongside Navar and four others, leaving Kaladin to witness the death of his friends.
Hab is a soldier serving in Kaladin's squad who survives an initial attack but is subsequently killed by lighteyed officers alongside his comrades Alabet, Reesh, and Coreb.
Reesh is a member of Kaladin's squad who is present during the execution scene where he is cut down first.
Alabet is a member of Kaladin's squad who, along with Hab, Reesh, and Coreb, is killed by lighteyed officers while attempting to defend himself with a knife.
Coreb is a character who addresses Kaladin as a Shardbearer and presents him with an object, which Kaladin subsequently returns to him.
A military encampment where thousands of soldiers line up in waves, serving as a central location for camp politics and troop organization.

An ideological group mentioned alongside heretics, Vorins, and Maakians as being equally closed-minded to Jasnah, who argues that true wisdom requires acknowledging the virtue in adversaries regardless of their affiliation.
Mungam is an individual referred to by Shallan as a 'tyrant' who is currently being argued with by Kabsal in the presence of a nurse.
Allahn is the author of a ketek poem that is frequently quoted in reference to Urithiru, though some mistakenly believe the poem indicates the Radiants' birthplace.
A type of spren that Syl encounters which does not pull her downward like other spren, allowing her to walk normally along the chasm wall.

Extes is a man who was subjected to a supernatural curse involving the daily torture of having his arms torn off, a fate he endured after sacrificing his son to the Voidbringers in exchange for knowledge of his death date.
Rianal is a distant western location from which the bridgeman Drehy originates, as indicated by his faint accent.
Varth is a soldier and squad leader who commands a group of bridgemen and is the target of Kaladin's vengeance.
A title given to Kaladin by his men, referring to a past version of himself who possessed confidence and strength, which he fears he may no longer be able to reclaim.
Lanacin the Surefooted is a person mentioned by the narrator as an individual who killed a Shardbearer, serving as a comparison to Evod Markmaker.
Evod Markmaker is a Shardbearer mentioned by the narrator as one of the few individuals who has killed a Shardbearer, alongside Lanacin the Surefooted.
Restares is a person whose name is invoked by Amaram as the correct choice for a difficult decision regarding the Shards, representing a course of action deemed necessary for the good of Alethkar.
Baxil is a character who works alongside Av, a confident Emuli man, as they move through a lavish palace corridor carrying a bag of tools.
Emuli is a term used to describe a specific physical appearance characterized by dark skin and hair, which Av possesses, though the text notes that not everyone with these features is Emuli.
Makabaki is a term used to describe a person from southwestern Roshar, characterized by dark skin and black hair, as exemplified by the bridgeman Sigzil.
Ashno of Sages is a wealthy and holy man who resides in a palace in Emul, employing hundreds of servants to maintain his home.
Sages is the name of a location, specifically the home of Ashno, a wealthy and holy man in Emul.
Emul is the kingdom containing the ancient city of Sesemalex Dar, which is home to Ashno of Sages, one of the richest and holiest men in the region.
The Hallowed Hall is a location within a lavish palace where wealthy individuals place images of their Kadasix for reverence, featuring artwork such as a gold leaf painting of Epan, Lady of Dreams.
Kadasix refers to the specific images or portraits of a wealthy man that are placed in the Hallowed Hall for reverence.
Epan is the subject of a painting depicting the Lady of Dreams, a piece of art displayed in the Hallowed Hall for reverence by a wealthy man.

Geranid is a woman who works on equations and studies spren in a small stone room on a tiny Reshi island, where she measures flamespren with calipers while her companion Ashir experiments with cooking.
The Physical Realm is the state of existence where the body must be fed, distinct from the state in which the spirit exists.
Shadesmar is a location visited by travelers where beings appear as they see themselves, and it is considered dangerous to visit with only a single dim sphere.

Gom is a person who brought a Shin vegetable to Ashir, which Ashir found fascinating and noted for its caramelization. Ashir expresses pleasure that Gom provided the vegetable and asks the listener to review his notes regarding the figures.
The Reshi Sea is a warm body of water dotted with tropical islands and inhabited by greatshell monsters, distinguished from the shallower Purelake.
Vevanev is a date marking the year 1173 and the time 107 seconds before a death, recorded in a document concerning an out-of-work Veden sailor.
Leef is a character who was dozing and was addressed by a tall, red-haired man with incredulity.
Feverstone is the name of a red-rock fortification located within a natural rift, featuring blocky, utilitarian buildings and square raincatchers that can withstand a siege for years.
The Order of the Stonewards is a group of individuals who are currently on foot, accompanied by a large number of Windrunners.
Mashala is a character addressed by Adolin and Renarin, whom Adolin refers to as his favored aunt and who uses a fabrial on her shoulder.
Vevahach is a prostitute who died on the 11th of 1173, eight seconds before the date was recorded.
A creature that crawls around injured individuals, appearing alongside Kaladin and Bridge Four as they encounter a wounded man from Eight.
Gibletish is a name suggested by a speaker for a Dysian Aimian, a creature created by combining a man's emotions and parts back together.
Ialai is a curvaceous woman who reportedly dyed her hair, a trait indicating foreign blood in her family's past, as Alethi hair always breeds true.
Au-nak is a character who speaks with an airy accent and argues that money is the true driver behind every war, while religion serves only as an excuse or justification.
Nak-ali is a character addressed with the high form of Au-nak's name by Hatham, who distinguishes between an excuse and a justification in their conversation.

Sesemalex Dar is the capital city of the kingdom of Emul, described as an ancient and spectacular trade city filled with rifts cut into the stone, which is the subject of a war between the Emuli and the Tukari.

The Dawncities are specific locations featuring unique stone compositions that allow for water drainage, a design attributed to the Dawnsingers. They are a subject of study and discussion among the lighteyes regarding their origins.
Tezim is the god-priest who leads the Tukari, a faction that claims the city's pattern is their ancestral homeland and a gift from the Heralds.
The Order of Talenelat is a devotary for the lesser people, a religious group associated with the worship of the Ten Heralds from ancient Vorin theology.

Brightlord Hatham is a highprince who instructs an ardent to insult Au-nak in order to make the foreigner believe he is shamed, thereby preventing suspicion regarding the speed of a contract signing. He is one of three highprinces, alongside Thanadal and Vamah, who are noted for being well behind in their payments.

A group of lighteyed officers who serve as personal bodyguards to Highprince Dalinar and Highprince Adolin, stepping up behind them during battle and maintaining a safe distance while their leaders fight.
A group or alliance that Dalinar is considering abandoning, as questioned by Sadeas regarding its current status.
Palahevan is a beggar of some renown, known for his elegant songs.
Brightking Alazansi is a ruler associated with a fleet of one hundred ships, as referenced by the character Leyten.

Jezrien is the deity worshipped by the people of Sesemalex Dar, the capital of the kingdom of Emul, who regard him as their only god rather than a figure from the Vorin religion. The city and kingdom are said to have been named by Jezrien himself.
Jezerezeh'elin is an alias for the Stormfather, who served as the king of the Heralds and was known for his mastery of storms, water, and life.
Nalma is a fellow slave who was lost by the protagonist during a previous escape attempt when the group was caught by their master's hunters.
Palahakev is the date of a document or record, specifically noted as 1173, 16 seconds pre-death, associated with a Thaylen sailor.
Arik is a member of the bridge crew whose death, along with Maps's, reduced the group to twenty-six members, causing significant difficulty in keeping up with other crews.

Derethil was a powerful king during the shadowdays who commanded thousands of soldiers and fought the Voidbringers during the days of the Heralds and Radiants. After finding peace unsatisfying, he commissioned a majestic ship intended to sail the seas during a highstorm.

The Wandersail is a ship that was nearly destroyed in a crash but survived with its crew, including Derethil and his sailors, who later found themselves stranded on a ring of islands. After being repaired by a strange people known as the Uvara, the ship was stormed by Derethil and his men, who then escaped with their guide, Nafti.
The Wander-sail is a vessel named by Derethil and crewed by the bravest sailors, which cast off into the ocean to seek the origin of the Voidbringers on a day when a highstorm brewed.

The Great Abyss is a faction of people known as Uvara, who are described as being prone to astonishing cruelty and slaughtering their own members for the slightest mistake to ensure their emperor does not suffer failure.

The Uvara is a group of people living on an island who, upon seeing a dead body, descend into chaos by burning homes and rioting. They possess shipyards where the Wandersail is repaired and are led by a guide and caretaker who joins Derethil's men during an escape.
Nafti is a female guide and caretaker who joins the crew of the Wandersail after begging to accompany Derethil and his men during their escape from the Uvara islands.
Shephir is the Midnight Mother, a terrifying entity capable of giving birth to abominations through her dark and consuming essence.

Shashabev is a dark-skinned dock-worker in his forties and father of three who bears three brands on his forehead, including a fresh one indicating he is dangerous. He is also known by the alias 'Shash' and is identified in a dated entry as being eight seconds from death.
The Outer Market is a disorganized collection of ramshackle stone buildings located near the king's palace, outside the warcamps, where an eclectic mix of people and Thaylen merchants gather.

Jakamav is a sturdy lighteyes of the third dahn who serves as an accompaniment for Inkima during an afternoon meeting with Adolin. He expresses a shared dislike for being wrong and engages in conversation regarding the time spent around Sadeas's lighteyes.
Eshava is a lighteye from Highprince Aladar's camp who accompanies Toral and is present in House Kholin's encampment, where she expresses dissatisfaction with Highprince Aladar's restrictions.
Inkima is a character who shares a moment of laughter with Jakamav before a serving maid arrives with wine.
Brightlord Resi is a noble addressed by Danlan in a conversation regarding a potential response to vocal provocations from Thanadal.
Bajerden is an alias for the holy man Nohadon, whose real name is unknown and was likely lost in time.
Natanatan is the Granite Kingdom, a legendary region in the East of Roshar characterized by feral highstorms, unclaimed hills, desolate plains, and overgrown forests.
Resi is the only full Shardbearer in Highprince Thanadal's army and serves as the Royal Defender of Thanadal's princedom, holding a Shardblade that he uses to duel Adolin.
Lady Istow serves as the highjudge who oversees a combat bout between Resi and Adolin, calling for the combatants to summon their Blades.
Stonestance is a specific fighting stance used by Resi that focuses on delivering maximum momentum and strength behind each strike, relying on parrying postures to defend against attacks.

The blade and plate are a set of ceremonial armor and weapon traditionally lent to highprinces or the king, representing a strong tradition within the Alethi culture that even King Gavilar respected despite his private complaints.
Betabanan is the date of a specific entry in a record, noted as 1173, occurring 45 seconds before the death of a lighteyed child of five years.
Chasmfiend is a specific term used by Kaladin to refer to a creature or entity associated with a chasm, though the provided text does not describe its nature or origin.
Betabanes is a date marking the year 1173, recorded as the time of a scholar's death.

Longshadow is the creator of a specific early model of a device designed to dull pain, which Navani used as a basis for her own refined invention. The text identifies Longshadow as the source of the original creations that Navani worked backward from to improve the design for medical use.

Karm is a person addressed by a regal man during a conversation about unconventional thoughts and divine election. The man refers to Karm's face as showing disagreement and notes that Karm uses his own sayings against him.
Surgebinders are a specific type of fabrial, a magical device, that are distinct from the bridge crews used by Highprince Dalinar's force.
Tarma is a location that is expected to be destroyed or overrun by the Desolations, alongside the cities of Sur and Eiliz, due to the high casualty rates from eleven years of war.
Eiliz is a kingdom that has been devastated by the Desolations, with its survival uncertain alongside Tarma due to the high number of fallen people.
Alakavish is a figure blamed by others for bringing the group to war before the Desolation, though he is described as a symptom of a greater disease rather than the sole cause of their suffering.
Corvana is the title of a volume written by an author named Corvana, which Navani retrieves from a shelf and examines on a writing desk.
The Silent Mount is a location in Jah Keved where the Vanrial, an order of artists, reside and sing ancient songs believed to be from the Dawnchant.

The Dawnchant is an ancient script containing songs written by the Heralds, which are claimed to have been sung by the Vanrial order of artists on the Silent Mount in Jah Keved, though the original meanings of the words have been lost.
Kakanev is the name of a city located in the Stormlight Archive universe, as indicated by the date 'Dated Kakanev, 1173' associated with a city guards-man.

The Desolations are a mysterious subject of inquiry for Dalinar and Renarin, described as a force connected to the Knights Radiant and the Almighty that the current generation lacks proof regarding its origin.

Parasaphi is a historical figure mentioned in a myth alongside Nadris, who is credited with searching for seed-stones. Her people were wiped out by the Desolations, an event referenced in the story's beginning.

Nadris is a historical figure mentioned in the myth of Parasaphi and Nadris, who died on his deathbed after Parasaphi brought stones touched by the Heralds to him. Following his death, Parasaphi harvested his seed to bring life to the stones, resulting in ten children who founded a new nation called Marnah.

Dara is a mountain range that serves as the mythical peaks where stones touched by the Heralds were found to repopulate a fallen people. These stones were brought to Nadris, whose seed was harvested to create ten children that founded the nation of Marnah.
Marnah is the name of a nation founded by Navani, who used ten children hatched from stones touched by the Heralds to repopulate her fallen people after harvesting the seed of Nadris.

A Shin Shardbearer is an individual who wields a Shard and wears Shard-plate armor, capable of using specialized weapons like grandbows that require such power to operate. This specific Shin Shardbearer is noted for assassinating the king of Jah Keved while wearing white clothing.
Avarak Matal is a man identified by Sadeas as looking familiar to Kaladin, though the provided text does not specify his role, origin, or relationship to the other characters.
Bridge Eighteen is one of the twenty bridge crews assigned to the operation, standing alongside Bridge Seven to observe Kaladin and his team.
Tarah is a person whom the speaker considers a failure alongside his other losses, specifically noting that she previously coaxed him out of his single-minded dedication.
Shshshsh is a character who is described as being loved by everyone, causing Navani to feel only mild jealousy rather than hatred.

The Black Fisher is a divine entity invoked by the speaker to consume their sorrow and end their weeping. It functions as a supernatural force capable of absorbing deep emotional pain upon invocation.
Bridge six is a specific crossing point located on a staging plateau where soldiers, including Kaladin's squad, are ordered to cross by Matal.
Dalinar's plate is a piece of armor that leaked Stormlight from dozens of small breaks, causing light to rise above it like steam from a cauldron and slowly diffuse.
A name shouted by groups of archers on a plateau, causing them to freeze and begin chanting a song in confusion.
Nelda is a person who is listed among the individuals Kaladin fears will die in the immediate future.
Tashlin is a Brightlord who commands a military company, and his reported death prompts concern among the soldiers regarding the weakening of the east side.
Sheler is the name of a character whose company is positioned on the southeast side of a hill and is currently holding its ground against enemy assaults during the battle.
Shesh lerel is the name of a banner displayed on the southeast side of a hill, which identifies the unit as Sheler's company.
Havar is a character who serves as the commander of the rear guard, a role confirmed by a man who calls out to him while riding with others.
Nacomb Gaval is a character who appears to be younger than Kaladin.
Malan is the title held by a high-ranking military leader who commands soldiers and is addressed by Adolin as 'Captainlord' while he attempts to rescue his father.
Tanatanev is a darkeyed mother of four in her sixty-second year who is recorded as being 18 seconds from death in the year 1173.
Navani is the widow of King Gavilar and the mother of Elhokar and Jasnah, who led research to develop grandbows using fabrial science.
Makal is an attendant who carries a satchel containing Navani's possessions, including her burn ink and a brushpen.

Elthal is a distinguished officer who survived a disaster and served as a gap holder alongside Dalinar during the final part of the fight. He is currently injured with his arm in a sling and is being addressed as a Brightlord.
A specialized fighting style or discipline that Szeth utilizes, which, when combined with Stormlight, makes him dangerous even without a Shardblade.
Vallano is the surname of Szeth, a man who killed Taravangian's friends and whom the king claims to share a heart with despite their violent history.
The Lifebrother is a figure referenced by a speaker who claims to speak the listener's language and suggests the possibility that this entity sent the listener to them.
Szeth-son-son-Vallano is a man addressed by Taravangian as a fellow sinner who personally holds knives and releases blood, with whom Taravangian claims to share one heart.

The True Desolation is a catastrophic event feared by the figures in the text, which is described as something that could happen to the whole kingdom and all of Roshar, and is explicitly stated as what 'he' wants.
The Ghostbloods are a specific group searching for secrets, identified by a symbol tattooed on the inside of the arm of their member Kabsal.

The Night of Sorrows is a catastrophic event associated with the Everstorm and the True Desolation, described as a time when the sun approaches the horizon and the world faces unity in the face of this coming darkness.
Talenel'elin is the name of a man who identifies himself as Stonesinew and Herald of the Almighty, who believes he has failed as the Desolation has arrived.
Stonesinew is a title held by Talenel'Elin, who identifies himself as the Herald of the Almighty. He believes he has failed as the Desolation has arrived.
